items.text: Quick reminder! The Riverside Campus Garden reopens this Saturday, March 22. Open hours are Tuesdays and Saturdays, 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM. Bring closed-toe shoes. Tool rental is $3 per session. So excited to see everyone back!

questions.stem: According to the post, how much does tool rental cost?
questions.explanation: The post states tool rental is $3 per session.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the garden's recent status?

options.A: It has been closed because of a budget shortage
options.B: It has lost most of its regular weekend volunteers
options.C: It has been closed for the winter season
options.D: It has changed its location to a different campus area

questions.explanation: The author says the garden "reopens this Saturday" in March, implying it was closed during winter.

items.text: Hi Maya, I'm confirming your campus tour guide shift for Friday, April 11, from 1:00 to 3:00 PM. Please arrive at the Visitor Center, Room 102, by 12:45 PM. Bring your staff badge. Thanks for volunteering! Best, Ms. Reyes

questions.stem: According to the email, what time should Maya arrive at the Visitor Center?
questions.explanation: The email instructs Maya to arrive at the Visitor Center by 12:45 PM.

questions.stem: What item must Maya bring to her shift?
questions.explanation: The email asks Maya to bring her staff badge.

items.text: Library Spring Break Hours: From March 24 to March 28, the Main Library will close at 6:00 PM each weekday and remain closed on the weekend. The 24-hour study room on the second floor will stay open with valid student ID access.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what time will the Main Library close during spring break weekdays?
questions.explanation: The notice states the library will close at 6:00 PM each weekday.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who want to study late during spring break?

options.A: They must reserve a study space in advance
options.B: They will need to use facilities on a different campus
options.C: They can use the 24-hour study room with their ID
options.D: They cannot study on campus after six o'clock

questions.explanation: The notice mentions the 24-hour study room stays open with valid student ID access.

items.text: Bike Rack Removal Notice: Racks outside Whitman Hall will be removed from April 7 to April 18 for sidewalk repair. Use the racks at Bryant Hall during this period. Bikes left at Whitman after April 7 will be impounded; a $25 release fee applies.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how much is the bike release fee?
questions.explanation: The notice states a $25 release fee applies to impounded bikes.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who use the Whitman bike racks daily?

options.A: They must apply for a new parking permit
options.B: They should temporarily relocate their bikes to Bryant Hall
options.C: They are allowed to leave their bikes locked overnight
options.D: They will receive a refund if their bikes are impounded

questions.explanation: The notice directs riders to use the racks at Bryant Hall during the repair period.
